meta.title: Purview Compliance Map

coverage_levels.Direct Support: Purview directly implements/enforces the control activity (within the stated scope).
coverage_levels.Partial Support: Purview implements part; a non-Purview control is also required.
coverage_levels.Evidence Support Only: Purview supplies evidence; the control itself lives elsewhere.
coverage_levels.Not Covered: Purview does not address this; another tool or process owns it (boundary row).

confidence_levels.High: Mapping is well-established and expected to withstand auditor scrutiny.
confidence_levels.Medium: Reasonable mapping; acceptance may vary by assessor/scope.
confidence_levels.Low: Defensible but narrow or contested; position carefully.

meta.disclaimer: Purview supports or evidences controls; it does not by itself make an organization compliant with any framework. Control references are practical intent mappings in original words, not quotations of the standards. Licensing claims derive from the Microsoft Purview service description per-feature tables and can change; verify at engagement time.

Information Protection.scope: Sensitivity labels, label-based encryption, content marking, Double Key Encryption, Customer Key

Data Classification.full_name: Microsoft Purview data classification (SITs, trainable classifiers, EDM, explorers)
Data Classification.scope: Sensitive information types, trainable classifiers, exact data match, Data explorer / Content explorer (classic), Activity explorer

Data Loss Prevention.scope: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive/Teams, Endpoint DLP, browser & network DLP, on-premises scanner, DLP for AI

Insider Risk Management.scope: Risk policies (data theft/leaks), sequence & anomaly detection, forensic evidence, Adaptive Protection

Communication Compliance.scope: Detection and remediation of policy-violating messages across Exchange, Teams, Viva Engage, Copilot

Information Barriers.scope: Segment-based restriction of two-way communication/collaboration in Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive

Data Lifecycle Management.scope: Retention policies and labels, adaptive scopes, inactive mailboxes, archiving

Records Management.scope: File plan, record/regulatory-record declaration, event-based retention, disposition review, proof of disposal

eDiscovery.scope: Unified eDiscovery: searches, holds, cases; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics). Classic experiences retired Aug 2025

Audit.scope: Unified audit log; Premium: 1-yr default retention (10-yr add-on), crucial events (MailItemsAccessed), higher API bandwidth

Compliance Manager.scope: Regulatory assessment templates, improvement actions, continuous control testing, compliance score

DSPM.full_name: Microsoft Purview Data Security Posture Management
DSPM.scope: Data risk discovery/analytics across M365 + Azure/Fabric/third-party (current version); DSPM (classic) for M365

DSPM for AI.full_name: Microsoft Purview DSPM for AI (classic; converging into DSPM)
DSPM for AI.scope: Visibility and policy for AI interactions: M365 Copilot, agents, Entra-registered and third-party AI apps

sspa-dpr.name: Microsoft SSPA DPR
sspa-dpr.full_name: Microsoft Supplier Data Protection Requirements (Supplier Security and Privacy Assurance program)

compliance_manager_template.note: No Compliance Manager template — supplier program requirements, not a public regulation. Verified absent from the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16.

sspa-dpr.applies_to: Suppliers processing Microsoft Personal Data and/or Microsoft Confidential Data (SSPA-enrolled)
sspa-dpr.notes: v12 (63 requirements) is current for FY26. Sections B, C, G, H and K carry no defensible Purview mapping (contractual/organizational or supplier-AI-system controls) — deliberate exclusions, see AUDIT-FINDINGS.md.

iso-27001-2022.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
iso-27001-2022.full_name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Information security management systems — Requirements (Annex A controls)
iso-27001-2022.version: 2022 (third edition; Amd 1:2024 does not alter Annex A)

compliance_manager_template.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16 (ISO/IEC 27001:2013 template also still offered).

iso-27001-2022.applies_to: Organizations certifying or aligning an ISMS to ISO/IEC 27001
iso-27001-2022.notes: Mapping covers Annex A controls where Purview has a genuine role. ISMS clause-level requirements (4–10) are management-system obligations and are deliberately unmapped except where Compliance Manager supplies evidence.

soc-2.name: SOC 2
soc-2.full_name: SOC 2 — 2017 Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy (With Revised Points of Focus — 2022), AICPA TSP Section 100

compliance_manager_template.name: System and Organization Controls (SOC) 2 (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16.

soc-2.applies_to: Service organizations undergoing SOC 2 examinations (criteria selected per engagement scope)
soc-2.notes: SOC 2 controls are entity-defined; these rows map where Purview capabilities commonly serve as the control activity or its evidence. Trust-category applicability varies by engagement.

hipaa-security.name: HIPAA Security Rule
hipaa-security.full_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164, Subpart C (Security Standards for the Protection of Electronic Protected Health Information)
hipaa-security.version: Current rule (2003, as amended through the 2013 Omnibus Rule); verified against eCFR 2026-07-14
hipaa-security.authority: US Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights

compliance_manager_template.name: HIPAA/HITECH (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16.

hipaa-security.applies_to: Covered entities and business associates handling electronic protected health information (ePHI)
hipaa-security.notes: Direction of travel: the Jan 6, 2025 NPRM (90 FR 898) proposes making encryption and MFA mandatory, adding asset inventories and 72-hour restore requirements. Not final as of 2026-07-16 (OMB Unified Agenda targets ~July 2027) — rows below map the rule in force. Privacy Rule crossovers (minimum necessary §164.502(b), de-identification §164.514, accounting of disclosures §164.528) are v2 backlog.

nist-800-171.name: NIST 800-171 R2 / CMMC L2
nist-800-171.full_name: NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 — Protecting CUI in Nonfederal Systems (assessed at CMMC 2.0 Level 2 per 32 CFR Part 170)
nist-800-171.version: SP 800-171 Rev 2 (Feb 2020, incl. errata). CMMC: 32 CFR 170 (eff. Dec 2024) + 48 CFR rule (clauses from Nov 10, 2025; Phase 2 C3PAO certifications from Nov 10, 2026)

compliance_manager_template.name: NIST 800-171 (premium); CMMC v2 Level 1 / Level 2 (premium; included by default for GCC/GCC High/DoD tenants)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16.

nist-800-171.applies_to: Defense industrial base organizations handling CUI (DFARS 252.204-7012/-7021); CMMC Level 2 scope
nist-800-171.notes: SP 800-171 R3 (May 2024) exists but is NOT the CMMC anchor — DoD's class deviation keeps R2. 3.13.11 (FIPS-validated cryptography) is deliberately unmapped: FIPS validation is a platform/module attribute argued from Microsoft's FIPS 140 documentation, not a Purview configuration claim. CUI enclaves are typically GCC High — cloud notes on every row; confirm tenant-specific feature status at engagement time.

nist-800-53.name: NIST 800-53 R5 (subset)
nist-800-53.full_name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 — Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations (curated data-protection subset)

compliance_manager_template.name: NIST 800-53 rev.5 (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Re-verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-17 (a NIST 800-53 rev.4 template also remains listed).

nist-800-53.applies_to: US federal agencies and systems (FISMA), FedRAMP-authorized cloud environments, and organizations adopting 800-53 voluntarily
nist-800-53.notes: Deliberate 21-control data-protection subset — not a catalog mapping (the catalog exceeds 1,000 controls and enhancements). Where a control's Purview story derives from an 800-171 requirement, the row cross-references it rather than restating the CUI framing. PT (privacy authority/consent/notice) and PM (program management) families were evaluated and excluded — process-owned, with Priva as the tooled layer (dependency only). Release 5.2.0 additions (SA-15(13), SA-24, SI-2(07)) are secure-software controls outside this subset. Federal context: GCC High is assessed against NIST SP 800-53 controls at a FIPS 199 High categorization (Microsoft 365 US Government service description) — every row carries a government-cloud note.

nist-csf-2.name: NIST CSF 2.0

compliance_manager_template.name: NIST CSF 2.0 (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16 (legacy NIST CSF 1.1 template also listed).

nist-csf-2.applies_to: Any organization using CSF 2.0 as its security program taxonomy (voluntary framework; outcome-based)
nist-csf-2.notes: CSF subcategories are outcomes, not prescriptive controls — coverage ratings read as 'Purview contributes this share of the outcome for the M365 data estate.' RC (Recover) and infrastructure-centric PR/DE subcategories are deliberately unmapped.

pci-dss-4.name: PCI DSS v4.0.1
pci-dss-4.full_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0.1
pci-dss-4.version: v4.0.1 (June 2024) — sole active version since Jan 1, 2025; all 51 future-dated requirements mandatory since Mar 31, 2025

compliance_manager_template.name: PCI DSS v4.0 (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16. Template pinned at v4.0; v4.0.1 added/deleted no requirements.

pci-dss-4.applies_to: Merchants and service providers storing, processing, or transmitting cardholder data (and orgs whose environment could impact its security)
pci-dss-4.notes: The M365 estate is typically out-of-CDE; keeping it that way is the point. DLP with the credit-card SIT is the canonical scope-reduction control for collaboration surfaces. CDE-internal requirements (network security, app security, key management) are deliberately unmapped.

glba-safeguards.name: GLBA Safeguards Rule
glba-safeguards.full_name: FTC Standards for Safeguarding Customer Information (GLBA Safeguards Rule), 16 CFR Part 314
glba-safeguards.version: As amended (2021 amendments fully effective Jun 9, 2023; breach-notification amendment effective May 13, 2024); verified against eCFR 2026-07-14

compliance_manager_template.name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, Title V, Subtitle A, Financial Privacy (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16. Template spans GLBA Title V; confirm safeguards-element coverage at engagement time. A separate 'FTC Privacy of Consumer Financial Information' template also exists.

glba-safeguards.applies_to: Non-bank financial institutions under FTC jurisdiction — lenders, advisors, fintech, auto dealers, mortgage/collections — plus Title IV higher-education institutions (FSA-enforced)
glba-safeguards.notes: Banking regulators (OCC/Fed/FDIC/NCUA) enforce parallel interagency guidelines for depository institutions — this mapping targets the FTC rule. §314.4(c)(5) MFA and (c)(4) secure development are deliberately unmapped (identity/SDLC territory).

ferpa.name: FERPA
ferpa.full_name: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act — 34 CFR Part 99 (implementing 20 U.S.C. § 1232g)
ferpa.version: 34 CFR Part 99, current text (verified against eCFR 2026-07-15 issue; base 53 FR 11943 as amended)
ferpa.authority: US Department of Education, Student Privacy Policy Office

compliance_manager_template.name: US - Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Re-verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-17.

domains: Subpart B — Right to inspect & review
ferpa.applies_to: Educational agencies and institutions receiving US Department of Education funds (K-12 districts and postsecondary institutions)
ferpa.notes: FERPA sets almost no prescriptive technical safeguards — §99.31(a)(1)(ii) itself frames the choice as 'physical or technological access controls' versus an effective administrative policy, which is exactly where the labeling/DLP story lands. Rows skew Partial/Evidence by design; the SIS and registrar processes remain the primary record store and system of record throughout. Watch item: ED signaled intent (Fall 2024 Unified Agenda) to propose FERPA regulation amendments — no NPRM published as of 2026-07-17.

gdpr.name: EU GDPR

compliance_manager_template.name: EU GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) (premium)
compliance_manager_template.note: Verified on the Compliance Manager regulations list 2026-07-16.

gdpr.applies_to: Controllers/processors handling EU residents' personal data (extraterritorial scope)
gdpr.notes: Microsoft Priva (Subject Rights Requests, Privacy Risk Management) is the tooled privacy layer and appears only as a dependency per ground rules. Rows annotated for reuse against US state privacy laws (CPRA and peers) pending the v2 composite.

healthcare.name: Healthcare & life sciences
healthcare.note: HIPAA Security Rule anchors; PCI for patient payments; state privacy composite on backlog.

dib.name: Defense industrial base
dib.note: CMMC Level 2 Phase 2 (third-party certification in new contracts) begins Nov 10, 2026. GCC High notes on every 800-171 row.

finserv.name: Financial services
finserv.note: SEC 17a-4 / FINRA supervision (strong Records Management + Communication Compliance stories) on v2 backlog.

higher-ed.name: Higher education
higher-ed.note: FERPA governs education records; Title IV institutions also answer to the GLBA Safeguards Rule (FSA-enforced). State student-privacy laws remain on backlog.

k12.name: K-12 education
k12.note: FERPA anchors; state student-privacy laws on backlog; CSF 2.0 is the working security baseline.

retail.name: Retail & e-commerce
retail.note: GDPR rows double as the analog for US state privacy (composite on backlog).

saas.name: SaaS & technology
saas.note: HIPAA applies when handling PHI as a business associate; PCI when in the payment path.

manufacturing.name: Manufacturing
manufacturing.note: 800-171/CMMC applies to DIB suppliers; GDPR for EU operations.

federal.name: US federal & FedRAMP-adjacent
federal.note: Federal-civilian (FISMA) agencies and SaaS providers pursuing FedRAMP. 800-53 mapped as a curated data-protection subset; GCC High is assessed against NIST SP 800-53 at FIPS 199 High. Distinct from the DIB/CMMC lens (800-171).

ms-supplier.name: Microsoft suppliers & partners
ms-supplier.note: SSPA enrollment drives DPR compliance; ISO 27001 certification can qualify for SSPA alternative-compliance paths.

rows.control_intent: Process Microsoft Personal Data only per Microsoft's documented instructions; keep instructions, policies, and procedures electronically accessible to staff on the engagement.
rows.capability_detail: Unified audit log of admin/policy changes; exportable policy configuration
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences what data-protection controls are configured and when they changed. The contractual instruction itself, and its accessibility to staff, are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Exports of label/DLP/retention policy configuration (Security & Compliance PowerShell)
rows.operational_evidence_example: Unified audit log extract of policy create/modify events over the review period
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Contract/DPA and SOW terms; policy management and document-control process
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention)

rows.control_intent: Monitor collection of Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data so that only data required to perform the engagement is collected (minimization).
rows.capability_detail: Data explorer / Content explorer (classic) baseline of sensitive data; DSPM posture trends
rows.how_it_supports: Surfaces where Microsoft personal/sensitive data accumulates across M365 so over-collection is visible and reviewable — implements the monitoring half of the requirement.
rows.config_evidence_example: SIT selection and label taxonomy scoped to Microsoft data categories
rows.operational_evidence_example: Recurring Data explorer export trended quarter-over-quarter; DSPM oversharing/risk reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Data-mapping exercise; collection controls in intake apps/forms
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Preserve reduced-identifiability of data sets received from Microsoft (pseudonymous/NPI/unlinked) — no re-identification.
rows.capability_detail: SITs detect direct identifiers; labels mark de-identified stores
rows.how_it_supports: Detecting direct identifiers appearing inside stores that should hold only de-identified data evidences that reduced identifiability is holding (or flags a breach of it).
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-labeling/DLP rules scoped to the de-identified data locations
rows.operational_evidence_example: DLP/auto-label alerts on identifier presence in those locations
rows.non_purview_dependencies: De-identification/anonymization process and tooling (primary); analytics environment controls
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Retain Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data no longer than needed to perform (or as law requires).
rows.capability_detail: Retention labels/policies with delete actions aligned to the engagement retention schedule
rows.how_it_supports: Automatically enforces maximum retention and deletion for Microsoft data held in M365 workloads. Data held outside M365 needs equivalent controls there.
rows.config_evidence_example: Get-ComplianceTag / retention policy export showing schedule-aligned periods
rows.operational_evidence_example: Deletion/disposition logs; Activity explorer retention-label events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Legal-approved retention schedule; retention controls for non-M365 repositories
rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher; Adaptive scopes / auto-apply retention: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: On completion (at Microsoft's discretion) return or securely destroy Microsoft data and keep a record of disposition.
rows.capability_detail: Disposition review (single/multi-stage) with records of disposition; retention label delete actions
rows.how_it_supports: The disposition workflow produces reviewable, exportable proof of destruction for M365-resident content. Return-of-data and physical media destruction are manual/organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: File plan with disposition-review settings on engagement data labels
rows.operational_evidence_example: Completed disposition records ('proof of disposal') exported from the portal
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Return-of-data procedure; physical media destruction and certificates
rows.license_requirement: Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Records Management incl. disposition review and proof of disposal: Available in GCC High; auto-apply record labels via trainable classifiers in development.

rows.control_intent: Assist Microsoft with data-subject rights: locate, access, correct, delete; record requests, actions, dates, and recipients of shared data.
rows.capability_detail: eDiscovery searches to locate/collect a subject's Microsoft Personal Data across Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive/Teams; Data explorer to scope where identifiers live
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the locate/collect step of DSR fulfilment for M365 content and evidences the search actions taken. Request intake, decisioning, and response records are process/tooling outside core Purview.
rows.config_evidence_example: eDiscovery case + search configuration for DSR fulfilment
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-request search/export records; DSR fulfilment log
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Microsoft Priva Subject Rights Requests (if licensed); privacy operations process; app-level data stores
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on

rows.control_intent: Maintain an incident response plan; notify Microsoft without undue delay on a Data Incident, cooperate with investigation (including scope of data accessed), remediate and track resolution.
rows.capability_detail: Audit (Premium) crucial events (e.g., MailItemsAccessed) + Activity explorer forensic timeline; DLP/IRM alerts as detection signal
rows.how_it_supports: Directly produces the 'scope of data accessed' artifact v12 requires during incident cooperation, plus the detection signals feeding the IR process. The plan, notification, and remediation tracking are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Alert policies enabled; audit retention configured (1-year default Premium)
rows.operational_evidence_example: Audit extracts and Activity explorer timeline captured during an incident; alert-to-ticket records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: IR plan and breach-notification process; SupplierWeb security contacts; SIEM/SOAR
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Perform annual security assessments (risks/vulnerabilities to confidentiality, integrity, availability of Microsoft Personal Data), review major changes, and keep change logs.
rows.capability_detail: Assessments with improvement actions and continuous control testing for the M365 estate; Purview admin audit as change log for data-protection policies
rows.how_it_supports: Structures and evidences the annual assessment activity for the M365 environment and records data-protection policy changes. Infrastructure vulnerability scanning and org-wide risk assessment are outside Purview.
rows.config_evidence_example: Active Compliance Manager assessment (e.g., Data Protection Baseline) with assigned actions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment report exports; unified audit log of policy changes with approver identity
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Vulnerability scanning/pen-test program; enterprise risk assessment; change-management system
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Account for all physical and virtual assets supporting the engagement, with owners; inventory includes data classification of the data on each asset, recovery and disposal records.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity labels as the classification dimension of the asset inventory; Data explorer as the data-on-asset view for M365
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the 'data classification of the data on the asset' element the v12 evidence bullet demands, for M365-resident data. The device/asset register itself is CMDB territory.
rows.config_evidence_example: Label taxonomy documentation; published label policies
rows.operational_evidence_example: Data explorer/Content explorer label distribution export mapped to the asset register
rows.non_purview_dependencies: CMDB/asset register; device lifecycle (recovery/disposal) records; Intune device inventory
rows.license_requirement: Manual sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (also F1/F3/Business Premium); analytics: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Access-rights management preventing unauthorized access to Microsoft data: identification, lockout, auto-logoff, strong credentials, MFA, least-privilege reviews, deactivation within 48 hours of termination.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption restricting who can open protected content regardless of location
rows.how_it_supports: Adds a content-layer access restriction on labelled Microsoft data — a narrow contributor. The requirement's substance (accounts, MFA, lockout, 48-hour deprovisioning, access reviews) is identity-platform territory.
rows.config_evidence_example: Label encryption permission configuration on top-tier labels
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted-item counts; access-denied events for protected content in audit log
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra ID, Conditional Access/MFA, PIM, access reviews, joiner-mover-leaver process (primary)
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier)

rows.control_intent: Run a DLP program: data classified, labelled, and protected; monitor systems processing Microsoft data for intrusions, loss, and unauthorized activity; program must include IDS/IPS, breach analysis, and offboarding communications.
rows.capability_detail: DLP across Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive/Teams + Endpoint DLP; sensitivity labels for the classify/label/protect chain; IRM + Adaptive Protection for unauthorized-activity monitoring
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements the classification/labeling/DLP core of the program, including egress blocking and endpoint/browser channels (covering staff use of third-party AI sites with Microsoft data). The mandated IDS/IPS layer and network monitoring are non-Purview.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP policy/rule export across workloads; Endpoint DLP settings; IRM policy configuration
rows.operational_evidence_example: DLP alert and user-override reports; IRM alerts; Activity explorer egress events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: IDS/IPS and network monitoring (Defender/SIEM); offboarding communications process
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); DLP for Teams: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Encrypt all engagement data in transit across networks with TLS or IPsec (per NIST 800-52/800-57); refuse unencrypted delivery; manage certificate lifecycle.
rows.how_it_supports: Purview does not implement or evidence transport-layer encryption or certificate management. Sensitivity-label encryption keeps protected files encrypted wherever they travel — complementary data-layer protection, but it does not support this control's transport requirement.
rows.non_purview_dependencies: TLS/IPsec configuration on services and network gear; certificate lifecycle management; M365 platform TLS (Microsoft-managed)
rows.license_requirement: n/a

rows.control_intent: Full-disk encryption on all supplier devices that access or handle Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data.
rows.how_it_supports: Disk encryption is implemented and evidenced by BitLocker/FileVault with Intune compliance reporting — not by Purview. Endpoint DLP requires onboarded devices but that is not evidence of disk encryption.
rows.non_purview_dependencies: BitLocker/FileVault via Intune; device compliance policy reports (primary)
rows.license_requirement: n/a

rows.control_intent: Encrypt at rest (per NIST 800-111) all Microsoft Personal/Confidential Data, including enumerated categories: credentials, payment data, medical, government IDs, DOB, geolocation, customer content, and more.
rows.capability_detail: Auto-labeling with label-based encryption targeting the enumerated categories via built-in SITs; Customer Key for the M365 at-rest encryption layer
rows.how_it_supports: Adds item-level encryption to at-rest sensitive content in M365 and lets the tenant hold the root keys (Customer Key). Nearly every enumerated v12 category has a built-in SIT for automatic targeting. Platform storage encryption (BitLocker/DEP in Microsoft datacenters) is the inherited base layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-labeling policies (simulation → enforce) scoped to enumerated-category SITs; label encryption settings; DEP/Customer Key configuration
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted/labelled item counts from Data explorer; Azure RMS usage logs
rows.non_purview_dependencies: M365 service encryption (inherited); encryption for non-M365 stores; Azure Key Vault key ceremony for Customer Key
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Anonymize Microsoft Personal Data before use in development or test environments — real personal data must not enter non-production.
rows.capability_detail: DLP/auto-label detection rules scoped to dev/test SharePoint sites and Teams; SIT hits as the violation signal
rows.how_it_supports: Detects (does not perform) anonymization failures: personal-data identifiers appearing in non-production locations raise alerts. The anonymization itself needs data-engineering tooling.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP rule scoped to dev/test locations with Microsoft-data SITs
rows.operational_evidence_example: Alert history for PII detections in non-prod; Data explorer view of non-prod locations
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Anonymization/synthetic-data tooling in the engineering pipeline (primary)
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)

rows.control_intent: Keep conflicting duties and areas of responsibility separated to reduce misuse or error.
rows.capability_detail: Segment-based blocking of two-way communication/collaboration between conflicted groups in Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive
rows.how_it_supports: Enforces communication/collaboration walls for conflict-of-interest scenarios (e.g., deal teams, research vs. trading) — a narrow, specific contributor. Role- and permission-level segregation of duties lives in the identity platform.
rows.config_evidence_example: IB segments and block policies for conflicted org units
rows.operational_evidence_example: IB policy application status; blocked-communication attempt records in audit
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra role design, PIM, access reviews for duty-level SoD (primary); org design
rows.license_requirement: Information Barriers: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on (restricted users need licenses)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Information Barriers: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Maintain an inventory of information and other associated assets with assigned owners.
rows.capability_detail: Data explorer / Content explorer (classic) and DSPM as the living inventory of sensitive information in M365
rows.how_it_supports: Produces and refreshes the information-asset half of the inventory (what sensitive data exists, where). Devices, systems, and ownership records belong to the CMDB/asset register.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Recurring Data explorer export; DSPM data risk assessment reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: CMDB/asset register with owners; process to reconcile data inventory to asset owners
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Define and enforce rules for acceptable use and handling of information and assets.
rows.capability_detail: Labels carry handling guidance (tooltips/descriptions); DLP policy tips nudge and enforce handling rules in the flow of work
rows.how_it_supports: Encodes the handling rules into the tools people use and interrupts violations at the point of action. The acceptable-use policy document and training are organizational.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Policy-tip shown/override telemetry from Activity explorer
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Acceptable-use policy; user awareness training
rows.license_requirement: Manual sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (also F1/F3/Business Premium); advanced policy tips: E5-tier (service description)

rows.control_intent: Classify information by protection needs (confidentiality, integrity, availability, stakeholder requirements).
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity label taxonomy as the org classification scheme; SITs/trainable classifiers for automated identification
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements information classification for the M365 estate: the label taxonomy is the classification scheme, applied manually and automatically.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Label distribution from Data explorer; auto-labeling simulation reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Classification policy document; data owners assigning classifications outside M365
rows.license_requirement: Manual sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (also F1/F3/Business Premium); Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Auto-labeling for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive, Office apps, and containers: Available in GCC High; named-entity SITs remain on engineering backlog in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Label information per the classification scheme so handling requirements are visible.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity labels apply visual marking (header/footer/watermark) and embedded metadata that travels with the item
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements labelling: persistent, machine-readable classification metadata plus human-readable markings on documents and emails.
rows.config_evidence_example: Content-marking configuration per label tier
rows.operational_evidence_example: Labelled-item counts and label-change events (Activity explorer)
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Labelling procedures for physical and non-M365 digital media
rows.license_requirement: Manual sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (also F1/F3/Business Premium)

rows.control_intent: Protect information in transfer — rules, procedures, and agreements for all transfer types.
rows.capability_detail: DLP rules on external sharing/egress across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, endpoints; label-based encryption keeping transferred files protected
rows.how_it_supports: Enforces the electronic-transfer rules for the M365 estate: blocks or conditions external sharing of sensitive content, and encrypts what does travel. Transfer agreements, physical transfers, and non-M365 channels sit outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP external-sharing rule set; labels with encryption for shareable tiers
rows.operational_evidence_example: External-sharing DLP alerts; override justifications
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Transfer agreements (legal); SPO/Teams external-sharing settings; Entra B2B configuration
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); DLP for Teams: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Assess information security events and decide whether they qualify as incidents.
rows.capability_detail: DLP/IRM alert queues with severity, evidence detail, and triage workflow; Activity explorer and Audit for event context
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the event detail, severity signal, and forensic context that the assessment decision consumes. The categorization/decision procedure itself is organizational (or SOC tooling).
rows.config_evidence_example: Alert policies with severity tiers; IRM triage configuration
rows.operational_evidence_example: Alert disposition history (dismissed/escalated) exported from alert queues
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Incident classification procedure; SIEM/SOAR case tooling
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); IRM signals: Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on

rows.control_intent: Establish procedures for identifying, collecting, and preserving evidence related to security events.
rows.capability_detail: Cases, holds, searches, and exports with defensible process; premium features add custodians, review sets, and advanced indexing
rows.how_it_supports: Implements identification, collection, and preservation of M365-resident evidence with an auditable chain of activity. The evidence-handling procedure and non-M365 sources are organizational/forensic-tooling concerns.
rows.config_evidence_example: eDiscovery case configuration with hold policy for investigation scope
rows.operational_evidence_example: Hold reports; search/export logs; case activity audit trail
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Forensic procedure and chain-of-custody policy; endpoint/network forensics tooling
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on
rows.cloud_availability_note: eDiscovery standard and premium features: Available in GCC High (a few premium items, e.g., Teams transcript collection, in development).

rows.control_intent: Identify and keep current the legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security.
rows.capability_detail: Regulation templates (370+ regulations) mapped to controls and improvement actions; template update flow when regulations change
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences a maintained, structured register of applicable requirements mapped to implementation actions for the M365 estate. Determining applicability is a legal/compliance function.
rows.config_evidence_example: Active assessments for the org's applicable regulations
rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment inventory export; template update acceptance history
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Legal register ownership; counsel review of applicability
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Protect records against loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access, and release, per retention requirements.
rows.capability_detail: Record and regulatory-record declaration (immutability), file plan, event-based retention, disposition with proof
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements records protection for M365 content: declared records resist edit/deletion, regulatory records lock entirely, retention enforces the schedule, and disposition produces proof.
rows.config_evidence_example: File plan export; record label configuration (record vs regulatory record)
rows.operational_evidence_example: Disposition records; record-locking events in audit
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Records governance policy; protection for physical/non-M365 records
rows.license_requirement: Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Records Management incl. disposition review and proof of disposal: Available in GCC High; auto-apply record labels via trainable classifiers in development.

rows.control_intent: Identify and meet requirements for privacy and protection of PII.
rows.capability_detail: PII-targeting SITs + Data explorer to locate PII; DLM retention aligned to privacy schedules; DLP guarding PII egress
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the technical layer: know where PII lives, retain/delete it on schedule, and prevent unauthorized egress. The privacy program (ROPA, DPIAs, subject-rights workflow) is organizational — Priva if tooled.
rows.config_evidence_example: PII SIT scoping; retention policies for PII stores; PII DLP rules
rows.operational_evidence_example: Data explorer PII distribution; retention/deletion logs; DLP alert history
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Privacy program and ROPA; Microsoft Priva (if licensed); DPO oversight
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher

rows.control_intent: Regularly verify compliance with the organization's own security policy, topic-specific policies, rules, and standards.
rows.capability_detail: Continuous assessment: automatic testing of technical improvement actions, compliance score trend, evidence upload for manual actions
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences recurring adherence checking for the data-protection control set in M365. Manual/organizational policy compliance still needs internal review.
rows.config_evidence_example: Assessment(s) mirroring internal policy baselines; automated testing enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Compliance score trend; improvement action status/history exports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Internal audit function; policy exception process
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Manage storage media through their lifecycle: use, transport, and secure disposal.
rows.capability_detail: Endpoint DLP controls copy of sensitive content to removable/USB media (audit, warn, block)
rows.how_it_supports: Restricts and logs the 'sensitive data onto removable media' slice of media handling on onboarded devices. Media inventory, transport, and physical destruction are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Endpoint DLP removable-media rules per label/SIT
rows.operational_evidence_example: Removable-media copy events and blocks from Activity explorer
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Media inventory and disposal process; device control (Defender for Endpoint); destruction certificates
rows.license_requirement: Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Protect information accessed on or processed by user endpoint devices.
rows.capability_detail: Endpoint DLP: egress controls on onboarded Windows/macOS devices (copy to USB/cloud/print/paste to browser)
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the data-egress dimension of endpoint protection. Device hardening, encryption, and management are the Intune/Defender layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: Endpoint DLP settings and device onboarding scope
rows.operational_evidence_example: Endpoint DLP activity and enforcement reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Intune device management + BitLocker; Defender for Endpoint
rows.license_requirement: Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Restrict access to information and other assets per the access-control policy.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption with usage rights (view/edit/print/forward) enforced wherever the item goes; DLP restricting access paths
rows.how_it_supports: Adds data-layer access restriction that follows the content beyond repository permissions. Identity, RBAC, and Conditional Access are the primary access-control plane.
rows.config_evidence_example: Label encryption permissions per tier
rows.operational_evidence_example: Access-denied/RMS usage logs; label distribution on restricted stores
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra ID, Conditional Access, PIM, SharePoint permission model (primary)
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier)

rows.control_intent: Delete information when no longer required, per policy and legal requirements.
rows.capability_detail: Retention policies/labels with delete actions; disposition workflow for reviewed deletion
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements scheduled, policy-driven deletion for M365 content, with proof via disposition records where review is configured.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention rule configuration with delete actions per schedule
rows.operational_evidence_example: Deletion events; completed disposition records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Retention schedule (legal); deletion for non-M365 stores

rows.control_intent: Mask data per policy so sensitive elements are hidden from unauthorized viewers.
rows.capability_detail: SIT detection reveals unmasked sensitive elements where only masked data should exist
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences whether masking is holding (or failing) in non-production and analytics stores — Purview performs no masking itself.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Detection findings/alerts for unmasked elements
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Masking/tokenization tooling in data platforms (primary); DB-level dynamic masking
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)

rows.control_intent: Apply data leakage prevention to systems, networks, and devices handling sensitive information.
rows.capability_detail: DLP across M365 workloads + Endpoint DLP + browser/network channels; Adaptive Protection tightening enforcement for risky users
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements DLP for the M365 estate, endpoints, and Edge — the control's namesake technology. Network-layer and non-M365 egress monitoring are complementary dependencies.
rows.config_evidence_example: Consolidated tiered DLP policy set export
rows.operational_evidence_example: DLP alerts, override and incident reports; Adaptive Protection risk-level changes
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Network egress controls; Defender for Cloud Apps for unsanctioned SaaS
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); DLP for Teams: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Produce, protect, and analyze logs of activities, exceptions, and events.

rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements logging for M365 user/admin/data activity with tamper-resistant, exportable records. Estate-wide logging (servers, network, apps) requires the SIEM layer.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Unified audit log extracts; retention policy inventory
rows.non_purview_dependencies: SIEM (Sentinel) for aggregation/correlation and non-M365 logs

rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Monitor networks, systems, and applications for anomalous behavior and act on findings.

rows.operational_evidence_example: IRM alert/case history; Adaptive Protection enforcement events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Sentinel/Defender XDR for network and system monitoring
rows.license_requirement: Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)
rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.

rows.control_intent: Define and implement rules for effective use of cryptography, including key management.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption (Azure RMS) with usage rights; Double Key Encryption for highest tiers; Customer Key for customer-held root keys
rows.how_it_supports: Implements content-layer encryption with policy-driven application and offers customer key control options. TLS, disk, and platform crypto plus enterprise key management are outside Purview.

rows.operational_evidence_example: RMS usage logs; encrypted-item distribution
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Key management (Azure Key Vault/HSM), TLS management, BitLocker; crypto policy document

rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Demonstrate commitment to integrity and ethical values (COSO Principle 1).

rows.license_requirement: Communication Compliance: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management/Compliance add-ons
rows.cloud_availability_note: Communication Compliance core: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Perform ongoing and/or separate evaluations of whether internal control components are present and functioning (COSO Principle 16).

rows.how_it_supports: Evidences a functioning ongoing-evaluation mechanism for the data-protection control set in M365. Entity-level and non-M365 control evaluations remain with internal audit.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Compliance score history; improvement-action test results export
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Internal audit program; management review cadence

rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Implement logical access security over protected information assets (identification, authentication, authorization, data security).

rows.operational_evidence_example: RMS usage/access-denied logs; label coverage reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra ID + Conditional Access + MFA + PIM (primary); network ACLs
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier)

rows.non_purview_dependencies: Media-sanitization process (NIST 800-88 aligned); asset disposal vendor certificates

rows.cloud_availability_note: Records Management incl. disposition review and proof of disposal: Available in GCC High; auto-apply record labels via trainable classifiers in development.

rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.operational_evidence_example: IRM alert/case exports; anomaly detections in Activity explorer

rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.

rows.control_intent: Information access management: authorize access to ePHI consistent with minimum-necessary principles (access authorization/establishment/modification).
