rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption on CUI items (auto-applied); Customer Key adding customer-controlled root keys to M365 service encryption; DKE for the most sensitive CUI
rows.how_it_supports: Implements item-level at-rest protection that persists beyond repository boundaries, layered on inherited M365 storage encryption. Endpoint disk encryption and non-M365 stores are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: CUI auto-labeling with encryption; Customer Key/DKE configuration where required
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted CUI item distribution from Data explorer; RMS logs
rows.non_purview_dependencies: BitLocker on endpoints (Intune); M365 service encryption (inherited); non-M365 store encryption
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.
rows.capability_detail: IRM behavioral analytics flagging unauthorized data use (unusual access, exfiltration sequences); Audit + Activity explorer as the review record
rows.how_it_supports: Identifies unauthorized *data* use inside the M365 enclave via behavioral detection. Network/system-level unauthorized-use detection is the Defender/SIEM layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: IRM policies with CUI-relevant indicators; alert thresholds tuned
rows.operational_evidence_example: IRM alert/case history; investigation records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Defender XDR/Sentinel for system-level detection; acceptable-use definitions
rows.license_requirement: Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on
rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.

rows.control_intent: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption with usage rights enforced wherever protected content travels; DLP constraining access paths
rows.how_it_supports: Adds data-layer authorization enforcement that follows the content beyond repository ACLs — the same contribution rated for ISO A.8.3 and SOC 2 CC6.1. Account-, role-, and system-level access enforcement is the identity/platform plane.
rows.config_evidence_example: Label encryption permissions on controlled-information tiers
rows.operational_evidence_example: RMS access-granted/denied logs for protected items
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra ID authorizations, Conditional Access, application ACLs (primary)
rows.license_requirement: Label-based encryption: included with sensitivity labeling entitlements (manual E3+; automatic application E5-tier)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Auto-labeling for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive, Office apps, and containers: Available in GCC High; named-entity SITs remain on engineering backlog in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Enforce approved authorizations for information flow within the system and between connected systems.
rows.capability_detail: DLP policies keyed to labels/SITs governing flows across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, endpoints
rows.how_it_supports: Implements content-aware flow enforcement for the M365 estate. Derived analog: the NIST 800-171 3.1.3 row carries the full CUI framing. Network-layer flow control (boundary devices, cross-domain solutions) is outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP rule set with authorized-destination exceptions per information type
rows.operational_evidence_example: DLP block/override reports for controlled flows
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Network segmentation and boundary flow control; cross-domain solutions
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); DLP for Teams: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Help authorized users decide whether to share information — matching a partner's authorizations to the information's restrictions — and support those sharing decisions.
rows.capability_detail: DLP policy tips surfacing restrictions at the moment of sharing; label metadata carrying access/use restrictions; sharing blocked where the restriction and recipient conflict
rows.how_it_supports: Implements decision support and enforcement at the point of sharing: users see the restriction, and out-of-policy shares are stopped or justified. Partner-authorization vetting is an organizational process.
rows.config_evidence_example: Policy tips enabled on sharing paths; label taxonomy encoding use restrictions
rows.operational_evidence_example: Tip-shown/override telemetry; blocked-share events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Sharing-agreement process; partner vetting; B2B collaboration configuration (Entra)
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); advanced policy tips: E5-tier (service description)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Control publicly accessible content: authorize posters, review proposed content, and keep nonpublic information off public systems.
rows.capability_detail: DLP blocking sensitive content in publicly shared M365 locations; DSPM oversharing assessments finding already-exposed content
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the technical guard and the detection sweep for public exposure in M365. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.1.22 row. Public websites/CMS review workflows are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP rule: sensitive SITs/labels + anyone-links → block; scheduled oversharing assessment
rows.operational_evidence_example: Public-exposure alerts; oversharing remediation reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Web publishing review procedure; CMS controls
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: Select the event types the system must log (AU-2) and generate audit records for them across system components (AU-12).
rows.capability_detail: Unified audit log generating records for hundreds of M365 operation types; Audit (Premium) crucial events (e.g., MailItemsAccessed)
rows.how_it_supports: Implements event selection and record generation for the M365 slice of the system boundary — combined into one row because the Purview story is uniform. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.3.1 row. Non-M365 components log via their own facilities.
rows.config_evidence_example: Audit enabled (tenant default); Premium licensing for crucial events confirmed
rows.operational_evidence_example: Audit record samples per event type for assessor testing
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Logging configuration on non-M365 components; SIEM collection (Sentinel)
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention); Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Review and analyze audit records for indications of inappropriate activity, and report findings.
rows.capability_detail: Audit search + Activity explorer as the review/analysis surfaces; DLP/IRM alerting as automated first-pass analysis of data-relevant events
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies the review tooling and the automated triage layer for M365 audit records. The documented review cadence, cross-system correlation, and reporting lines are organizational/SIEM.
rows.config_evidence_example: Documented review procedure referencing audit search/Activity explorer; alert policies enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Dated review notes with attached exports; alert disposition history
rows.non_purview_dependencies: SIEM correlation and dashboards; assigned reviewer roles
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention); Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.
rows.capability_detail: Service-managed, tamper-resistant unified audit store; role-gated audit search; retention policies preventing early truncation; audit-of-audit-access trail
rows.how_it_supports: The M365 audit store cannot be modified by tenant users and read access is role-scoped. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.3.8 row. Exported logs and SIEM copies need their own protection.
rows.config_evidence_example: Audit-access role assignments reviewed; retention policies locked to schedule
rows.operational_evidence_example: RBAC review of audit roles; audit records of audit-search activity
rows.non_purview_dependencies: SIEM-side log protection; export handling procedure
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Retain audit records for an organization-defined period consistent with the records-retention policy, to support after-the-fact investigations.
rows.capability_detail: Audit (Premium) 1-year default retention for core workloads; audit log retention policies by service/activity/user; 10-year retention with the add-on license
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements configurable, policy-driven audit retention for M365 records at the periods federal schedules typically demand. Non-M365 log retention is the SIEM/archive layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: Audit retention policies matching the approved retention schedule
rows.operational_evidence_example: Retention policy inventory; sample retrieval of aged records inside the window
rows.non_purview_dependencies: SIEM/archive retention for non-M365 logs; records schedule approval (e.g., NARA GRS alignment)
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Identify and document where designated information lives, which system components process and store it, and who has access.
rows.capability_detail: Data explorer / Content explorer (classic) continuously identifying sensitive-information locations across M365; DSPM adding exposure and access-path insight
rows.how_it_supports: The most Purview-shaped control in the catalog: for M365 components, Data explorer is the location documentation, live rather than point-in-time, and DSPM covers the who-has-access dimension. Non-M365 components need their own information-location tooling. CM-12(1) (automated location tools) is exactly this capability.
rows.config_evidence_example: SIT/classifier scope covering the designated information types; DSPM enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Data explorer location exports filed as CM-12 documentation; DSPM access/oversharing reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Information-location documentation for non-M365 components; data-flow diagrams
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance; DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Content/Activity explorer analytics: Available in GCC High (Teams data in Content explorer still in development).

rows.control_intent: Operate incident-handling capability covering preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
rows.capability_detail: DLP/IRM alerts as detection input; Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics; eDiscovery preservation of incident evidence
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies detection signals and the forensic/preservation evidence the handling capability consumes. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.6.1–3.6.2 row carries the full framing. The capability itself, playbooks, and US-CERT/agency reporting are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Alert policies on sensitive-data egress; audit retention across investigation windows
rows.operational_evidence_example: Incident packets combining alerts, audit timelines, and hold records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: IR plan and SOC processes; SIEM/SOAR; agency reporting obligations (FISMA/US-CERT)
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Respond to information spills: identify the spilled information and where it went, alert responders, isolate and eradicate it, and assess exposure.
rows.capability_detail: DLP/SIT detection identifying spilled sensitive information in unauthorized locations; eDiscovery search establishing spread and executing removal; Audit tracing who accessed the spill
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the identify/locate/trace core of spillage response for M365: detection raises the alert, search maps the spread, audit shows exposure, and targeted deletion supports eradication — the classic CUI-spillage-in-collaboration workflow. Sanitization decisions and system-level remediation are organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: DLP alerting across all workloads; documented spillage runbook using eDiscovery search + purge steps
rows.operational_evidence_example: Spillage case records: detection alert, spread map, access trace, removal verification
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Spillage response roles and procedures; system-level sanitization where required
rows.license_requirement: DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: eDiscovery standard and premium features: Available in GCC High (a few premium items, e.g., Teams transcript collection, in development).

rows.control_intent: Mark media to indicate distribution limitations, handling caveats, and security markings of the information.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity labels applying visual markings (headers/footers per marking scheme) and machine-readable designation to digital content
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements digital marking at scale, with the label doubling as the enforcement hook for DLP. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.8.4 row (CUI markings). Physical media marking remains manual.
rows.config_evidence_example: Label taxonomy with marking text per designation/caveat
rows.operational_evidence_example: Marked-item counts; label application events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Marking policy (e.g., CUI Registry alignment); physical media marking procedure
rows.license_requirement: Manual sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (also F1/F3/Business Premium); Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Auto-labeling for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive, Office apps, and containers: Available in GCC High; named-entity SITs remain on engineering backlog in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Sanitize system media before disposal, release, or reuse, using approved techniques proportional to the information's sensitivity.
rows.how_it_supports: Deliberate boundary row: media sanitization is a device/media-level control (NIST SP 800-88 techniques, degaussing, destruction) that Purview neither performs nor evidences. Purview retention-deletion and disposition remove *content* from live services — related, but not sanitization of media, and the atlas keeps that distinction honest (the 800-171 analog 3.8.3 was deliberately left unmapped in v1 for the same reason).
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Media sanitization program per NIST SP 800-88; destruction certificates; Microsoft datacenter media handling (inherited, evidenced via FedRAMP packages)
rows.license_requirement: n/a

rows.control_intent: Restrict or prohibit the use of specified media types on systems (e.g., removable/portable storage).
rows.capability_detail: Endpoint DLP removable-media rules (audit/warn/block) for sensitive content on onboarded devices
rows.how_it_supports: Implements content-aware removable-media restriction on managed endpoints. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.8.7 row. Port-level device control is the Intune/Defender layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: Endpoint DLP removable-media rules per label/SIT
rows.operational_evidence_example: Removable-media event and block reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Intune/Defender device control (port/device-class level)
rows.license_requirement: Endpoint DLP: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Endpoint DLP, Teams DLP, and on-premises scanner: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD (Purview GCC High deployment guide).

rows.control_intent: On termination: disable access and revoke credentials within defined timeframes, and protect organizational information during and after departure.
rows.capability_detail: Data-theft-by-departing-users policy elevating scrutiny of leavers' data activity; forensic evidence for investigations
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the information-protection monitoring slice of termination handling. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.9.2 row. Access disablement and credential revocation are identity-plane actions.
rows.config_evidence_example: IRM departing-user policy with HR connector or Entra deletion trigger
rows.operational_evidence_example: IRM alerts/cases tied to departure events
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Entra offboarding automation (disable, revoke, session kill); exit process
rows.license_requirement: Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on
rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.

rows.control_intent: Assess risk — likelihood and impact of unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction of the system and its information — and document, review, and update the assessment.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitive-data discovery (Data explorer) and DSPM data-risk analytics supplying the where-is-it and how-exposed-is-it inputs
rows.how_it_supports: Feeds the risk assessment its data-layer evidence base, the same pattern rated for HIPAA §164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) and GLBA §314.4(b). The assessment methodology, threat analysis, and documentation are program activities.
rows.config_evidence_example: SIT/classifier scope over assessed information types; DSPM assessments scheduled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Discovery and risk exports cited in the risk-assessment document
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Risk-assessment methodology (SP 800-30); system-level threat analysis
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance; DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Content/Activity explorer analytics: Available in GCC High (Teams data in Content explorer still in development).

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of transmitted information.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based and message encryption keeping protected content encrypted end-to-end in mail/sharing flows; DLP blocking unprotected transmission of sensitive content
rows.how_it_supports: Adds content-layer cryptographic protection to transmissions and stops the unprotected ones. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.13.8 row. Transport-layer crypto (TLS/IPsec, SC-8(1) mechanisms) is platform/network-owned — FIPS-validation arguments belong to Microsoft's FIPS 140 documentation, not Purview configuration.
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-apply encryption on protected labels; DLP rule blocking external sends without protection
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted-send logs; DLP alerts on unprotected attempts
rows.non_purview_dependencies: TLS/IPsec configuration; FIPS 140 module validation documentation (platform)
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.
rows.capability_detail: Auto-applied label encryption on at-rest sensitive content; Customer Key for customer-held M365 root keys; DKE for highest-sensitivity tiers
rows.how_it_supports: Implements item-level at-rest protection layered on inherited platform storage encryption. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.13.16 row. Endpoint disk and non-M365 store encryption are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-labeling with encryption; Customer Key/DKE configuration where required
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted-item distribution from Data explorer; RMS usage logs
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Platform storage encryption (inherited via FedRAMP authorization); BitLocker on endpoints (Intune)
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Monitor the system to detect attacks, indicators of potential attacks, and unauthorized use.
rows.capability_detail: IRM behavioral analytics flagging unauthorized data use; DLP alerts; Activity explorer as the data-plane monitoring record
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the unauthorized-data-use slice of system monitoring. Derived analog: the 800-171 3.14.7 row. Attack detection across network/hosts is the Defender/SIEM layer.
rows.config_evidence_example: IRM policies with relevant indicators; alert thresholds tuned
rows.operational_evidence_example: IRM alert/case history; monitoring review records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Defender XDR/Sentinel (attack detection, network monitoring); monitoring strategy
rows.license_requirement: Insider Risk Management: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Insider Risk Management add-on; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included)
rows.cloud_availability_note: IRM core policies (data theft by departing users, general data leaks, forensic evidence): Available in GCC High; analytics and several policy templates in preview/development.

rows.control_intent: Manage and retain information within the system, and information output from it, per applicable laws, regulations, policies, and operational requirements.
rows.capability_detail: Retention policies/labels enforcing schedule-aligned retention and deletion across M365; Records Management for declared records and disposition with proof; adaptive scopes for population-specific schedules
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements information management and retention for M365 content — the catalog's retention control, and the same Direct-rated story as GLBA §314.4(c)(6) and GDPR Art. 5(1)(e). Records-schedule approval (e.g., NARA GRS alignment for agencies) and non-M365 stores are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention policies/labels mapped to the approved records schedule; file plan for records
rows.operational_evidence_example: Retention/deletion event logs; disposition records
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Approved records schedule (NARA GRS for federal); retention for non-M365 systems
rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher; Adaptive scopes / auto-apply retention: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Records Management incl. disposition review and proof of disposal: Available in GCC High; auto-apply record labels via trainable classifiers in development.

rows.control_intent: Remove designated PII elements from datasets (de-identification) and evaluate on a defined frequency whether the de-identification holds.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Recurring zero-hit (or remediation) reports filed as the part-(b) evaluation
rows.non_purview_dependencies: De-identification tooling and technique selection (SP 800-188); dataset release process

rows.control_intent: Understand and manage legal, regulatory, and contractual cybersecurity requirements, including privacy obligations.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment inventory and update-acceptance history

rows.capability_detail: Data explorer / Content explorer (classic) as the continuously refreshed inventory of sensitive data with classification metadata; DSPM extending views across estate
rows.how_it_supports: Directly maintains the sensitive-data inventory (locations, types, labels, counts) for M365 — the subcategory's core outcome for that estate. Databases and non-M365 stores need their own inventory tooling.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Scheduled Data explorer exports; DSPM data inventory views

rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance; DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row)

rows.control_intent: Manage systems, hardware, software, services, and data throughout their life cycles.

rows.how_it_supports: Implements the *data* dimension of lifecycle management for M365 content end-to-end. System/hardware/software lifecycle is ITAM/CMDB territory.

rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher; Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest.
rows.capability_detail: Sensitivity labels with encryption on at-rest content (auto-applied by SITs/classifiers); Customer Key/DKE for key control; record immutability for integrity
rows.how_it_supports: Implements item-level at-rest confidentiality (and contributes integrity via record locking) for M365 data, layered on inherited platform encryption. Non-M365 stores and backup availability are outside.

rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted-item distribution; RMS usage logs

rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit.
rows.capability_detail: DLP governing what sensitive data may leave via email/sharing/upload; label & message encryption keeping permitted transfers protected end-to-end
rows.how_it_supports: Controls and protects sensitive data movement at the content layer for M365 channels. Transport encryption (TLS) is platform/network-managed.

rows.license_requirement: Audit (Standard): included across Microsoft 365/Office 365 enterprise, government, and business plans (180-day retention); Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license; Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher

rows.control_intent: Destroy personally identifiable information shared under the studies and audit/evaluation exceptions once it is no longer needed for that purpose.
rows.capability_detail: Retention labels with delete actions (or event-based retention keyed to study/audit end) on the M365 locations holding shared study or evaluation datasets; disposition review producing destruction evidence
rows.how_it_supports: Directly enforces the destroy-when-no-longer-needed clock for shared datasets that live in M365 (research collaboration sites, evaluation workspaces) and produces the destruction proof the written agreements call for. Agreement drafting and destruction in recipients' non-M365 environments are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention label with end-of-study delete action on study-data sites; disposition review enabled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Deletion/disposition records mapped to study or audit closure dates
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Written agreements specifying destruction terms (§99.31(a)(6)(iii)); recipient-side destruction attestation
rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher; Adaptive scopes / auto-apply retention: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Records Management (file plan, record declaration, disposition review): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Data minimisation: personal data must be adequate, relevant, and limited to what the purpose requires.
rows.capability_detail: Personal-data discovery (EU-relevant SITs, Data explorer) making accumulation visible; DSPM surfacing over-retained/overshared personal data
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences whether minimisation is holding in collaboration estates and gives the review a concrete target list. Minimisation decisions at collection points are application/process design.
rows.config_evidence_example: EU personal-data SITs scoped; recurring discovery
rows.operational_evidence_example: Trended personal-data footprint reports feeding minimisation reviews
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Collection-point design; purpose specification (privacy office)
rows.license_requirement: Data classification analytics (Data/Content/Activity explorer): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, E5 Compliance (Purview Suite), or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Storage limitation: keep personal data identifiable no longer than the purpose requires.
rows.capability_detail: Retention policies/labels enforcing purpose-aligned retention periods with automatic deletion; adaptive scopes for population-specific schedules
rows.how_it_supports: Directly implements storage limitation for personal data in M365 workloads. Retention schedule design and non-M365 stores are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Retention policies mapped to the ROPA's retention column
rows.operational_evidence_example: Deletion logs; retention policy inventory
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Approved retention schedule; application-store purges
rows.license_requirement: Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher; Adaptive scopes / auto-apply retention: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance

rows.control_intent: Accountability: the controller must be able to demonstrate compliance with the processing principles.
rows.capability_detail: GDPR premium assessment tracking technical/organisational measures; Purview policy configuration + audit trail as demonstrable-compliance artifacts
rows.how_it_supports: Supplies a standing, exportable demonstration layer (assessments, control status, policy evidence, activity logs) for the M365 processing estate. Accountability governance (DPO, ROPA, DPIAs) is organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Active EU GDPR assessment; policy exports retained
rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment reports; audit extracts evidencing control operation
rows.non_purview_dependencies: DPO function; ROPA; processor agreements
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Right of access: confirm processing and provide the data subject a copy of their personal data.
rows.capability_detail: eDiscovery search/collection locating a subject's personal data across Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive/Teams; export for response assembly
rows.how_it_supports: Implements locate/collect/export for M365 content in access-request fulfilment. Intake, identity verification, redaction of third-party data, and response are process/Priva.
rows.config_evidence_example: Documented DSR search procedure with eDiscovery case template
rows.operational_evidence_example: Per-request search/export records within the one-month clock
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Microsoft Priva Subject Rights Requests (if licensed); line-of-business system search
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on
rows.cloud_availability_note: eDiscovery standard and premium features: Available in GCC High (a few premium items, e.g., Teams transcript collection, in development).

rows.control_intent: Right to erasure: delete a subject's personal data without undue delay when grounds apply.
rows.capability_detail: eDiscovery locating all instances; retention/deletion actions and targeted purge for mail items; documented deletion evidence
rows.how_it_supports: Implements find-then-erase for M365 content with an evidence trail. Erasure-grounds assessment, exemption analysis, and app-database deletion are process territory; bulk purge in M365 has product limits worth flagging in design.
rows.config_evidence_example: Erasure runbook: eDiscovery search → per-location deletion steps → verification search
rows.operational_evidence_example: Before/after search results as deletion proof
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Priva SRR (if licensed); application-level deletion; backup-cycle policy statement
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher

rows.control_intent: Data portability: provide the subject's data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
rows.capability_detail: Collection/export of a subject's content in native formats (PST, native files, load files)
rows.how_it_supports: Supports the collection/export step; 'structured machine-readable' delivery for provided-data sets is largely an application/CRM export concern.
rows.config_evidence_example: Export procedure per format requirements
rows.operational_evidence_example: Export manifests per request
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Application-level structured exports; Priva
rows.license_requirement: eDiscovery (search, cases, hold, export): Microsoft 365/Office 365 E3-tier; premium features (custodians, review sets, analytics): E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on

rows.control_intent: Data protection by design and by default: embed technical/organisational measures (e.g., minimisation, pseudonymisation) into processing.
rows.capability_detail: Default sensitivity labels, auto-labeling, default-deny DLP posture, and default retention as by-design/by-default technical measures for the collaboration estate
rows.how_it_supports: Implements 'by default' concretely in M365: new content lands labelled, protected, retention-bound without user action. System/application design outside M365 is the broader obligation.
rows.config_evidence_example: Default label policy; auto-labeling; baseline DLP; default retention
rows.operational_evidence_example: Coverage metrics: % content labelled by default; policy inventory
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Privacy engineering in product/app design; DPIA-driven measures
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; DLP for Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher (Business Premium included); Org/location-wide retention policies & manual retention labels: Microsoft 365 E3/A3/G3 or higher

rows.control_intent: Maintain records of processing activities (purposes, categories, recipients, transfers, retention, security measures).
rows.how_it_supports: Deliberate boundary row: the ROPA is a governance register core Purview does not produce. Purview outputs (data inventory, retention configuration) feed ROPA fields, but the record itself is Priva/GRC/process tooling.
rows.non_purview_dependencies: ROPA tooling/process (Priva, GRC platform, registers); DPO ownership
rows.license_requirement: n/a

rows.control_intent: Security of processing: pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data as risk-appropriate measures.
rows.capability_detail: Label-based encryption on personal-data content (auto-applied); Customer Key/DKE options; SIT detection guarding pseudonymised sets against re-identification leakage
rows.how_it_supports: Implements the encryption measure for personal data in M365 and evidences pseudonymisation hygiene. Database-layer crypto and pseudonymisation engineering are outside.
rows.config_evidence_example: Auto-label + encrypt on personal-data tiers; Customer Key where adopted
rows.operational_evidence_example: Encrypted personal-data item distribution; RMS logs
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Application/database encryption; pseudonymisation tooling; key management
rows.license_requirement: Automatic/policy-based sensitivity labeling: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance; Customer Key: Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 Information Protection & Governance
rows.cloud_availability_note: Customer Key and Double Key Encryption: Available in GCC High.

rows.control_intent: Regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures.
rows.capability_detail: GDPR assessment with continuous automated control testing; DLP simulation-mode reports and policy-effectiveness analytics as measure-testing artifacts
rows.how_it_supports: Evidences a recurring effectiveness-evaluation mechanism for the M365 measure set. Penetration testing and org-wide evaluation are the broader program.
rows.config_evidence_example: GDPR assessment active with automated testing; DLP simulation reviews scheduled
rows.operational_evidence_example: Assessment score trend; simulation/effectiveness reports
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Pen-test program; audit function
rows.license_requirement: Compliance Manager: baseline included with Microsoft 365/Office 365 plans; premium regulation templates licensed separately (3 premium templates included at E5/A5/G5)
rows.cloud_availability_note: Compliance Manager: Available in GCC/GCC High/DoD; CMMC templates included by default for government tenants.

rows.control_intent: Notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours (with nature, categories, and approximate numbers of subjects/records), document all breaches (33(5)), and communicate high-risk breaches to data subjects.
rows.capability_detail: Audit (Premium) scope-of-access forensics quantifying categories and approximate numbers of data subjects/records concerned; eDiscovery preserving breach evidence; DLP/IRM alerts starting the 72-hour clock with timestamps
rows.how_it_supports: Produces precisely the notification-content facts Art. 33(3) demands and the documentation Art. 33(5) requires, inside the 72-hour window. Risk assessment, notification decisions, and filings are legal/organizational.
rows.config_evidence_example: Alert policies + audit retention across breach lookback windows
rows.operational_evidence_example: Breach workpapers: audit timeline, affected-subject estimates, evidence holds
rows.non_purview_dependencies: Breach counsel; supervisory-authority filing process; subject-communication mechanics
rows.license_requirement: Audit (Premium): Microsoft 365 E5/A5/G5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or E5 eDiscovery & Audit add-on; 10-year retention needs the add-on license
rows.cloud_availability_note: Audit (Standard/Premium) incl. 1-year and 10-year retention: Available in GCC High/DoD.

rows.control_intent: Conduct data protection impact assessments before high-risk processing (systematic description, necessity, risks, measures).
rows.capability_detail: DSPM data-risk views describing what personal data a processing surface touches and how exposed it is; DSPM for AI supplying the AI-processing visibility DPIAs for AI systems need
rows.how_it_supports: Feeds the DPIA's 'systematic description of processing and risks' with live estate evidence — especially for Copilot/AI-adoption DPIAs where DSPM for AI reports are the concrete data-flow record. The DPIA method, necessity test, and sign-off are the privacy office's.
rows.config_evidence_example: DSPM enabled over in-scope estates; DSPM for AI reports for AI DPIAs
rows.operational_evidence_example: DSPM/AI-activity reports attached to DPIA workpapers
rows.non_purview_dependencies: DPIA methodology and register; DPO consultation
rows.license_requirement: DSPM: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite (per DSPM get-started documentation; not yet a service-description row); DSPM for AI: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Suite; monitored Copilot users need Microsoft 365 Copilot licenses; some non-M365 sources are pay-as-you-go
rows.cloud_availability_note: DSPM for AI in GCC High/DoD: available for supported AI sites only; browse-to-URL policies cannot be created.
